selection_rule: A fifteen-chapter thesis-to-method route through the canonical manifest. It keeps the architecture thesis, efficiency and authority boundaries, failure and evidence discipline, oversight, governed improvement, the intent-to-effect trace, verification and artifact continuity, governance economics, integrated synthesis, and the living-book method. Specialized mechanisms remain available in the architecture reference.

chapters.reader_question: What changes when advanced AI is treated as a governed system of responsibilities instead of one opaque model?
chapters.running_example: Follow one repository change from a human request through planning, verification, authorized execution, evidence capture, and possible refusal.
chapters.strongest_objection: A sufficiently capable single model may perform every cognitive role internally, making a layered stack look like unnecessary bureaucracy.
chapters.failure_story: The repository change succeeds, but nobody can reconstruct who authorized it, which source constrained it, or how to reverse it.
chapters.evidence_that_would_change_the_conclusio: Matched deployments showing that uncontracted monolithic operation is as controllable, inspectable, and recoverable as enforced responsibility boundaries would narrow the thesis.
chapters.handoff: The next chapter asks whether those boundaries can improve capability without spending maximal cognition on every step.

chapters.reader_question: Can capability grow through governed routing and reuse without hiding the costs and failures displaced by cheaper cognition?
chapters.running_example: Compare a frontier-model-only repository change with a routed change that reuses context, specialists, tests, and independent verification.
chapters.strongest_objection: The verification and accounting needed to govern cheaper routes may cost more than simply using the strongest model throughout.
chapters.failure_story: A cheap route looks efficient because repair time, reviewer attention, and a missed constraint were excluded from its ledger.
chapters.evidence_that_would_change_the_conclusio: Matched workloads showing no risk-adjusted cost or quality advantage after full verification, repair, and human burden would reject or sharply narrow the efficiency hypothesis.
chapters.handoff: Efficiency becomes safe only after the architecture separates the ability to propose work from permission to create effects.

chapters.reader_question: How should the stack represent who may read, propose, approve, execute, deploy, revoke, or refuse?
chapters.running_example: The repository-change planner may draft a patch, but only a bounded adapter with a current grant may alter the working tree.
chapters.strongest_objection: Typed authority records can become ceremonial metadata if the runtime can ignore, forge, or bypass them.
chapters.failure_story: A low-authority planner persuades a broadly privileged tool to apply a change after the original grant has expired.
chapters.evidence_that_would_change_the_conclusio: Runtime traces showing that simpler session-level permission systems resist confused-deputy, stale-grant, and revocation attacks equally well would weaken the need for richer typed authority.
chapters.handoff: Once authority is explicit, the book can classify the quieter stack failures that occur before catastrophic scenarios.

chapters.reader_question: Which ordinary contract failures reveal ungoverned intelligence before a dramatic system-level disaster occurs?
chapters.running_example: Diagnose whether a bad repository change came from poisoned context, authority creep, verifier correlation, hidden residuals, or self-ratifying evidence.
chapters.strongest_objection: A broad failure taxonomy may explain every incident after the fact while predicting or preventing very little.
chapters.failure_story: A fluent postmortem labels the incident but assigns no detector, containment route, owner, or residual, so the same defect returns.
chapters.evidence_that_would_change_the_conclusio: Prospective evaluations showing that the taxonomy does not improve detection, containment, or recovery over a simpler incident model would require consolidation or rejection.
chapters.handoff: A failure taxonomy can govern decisions only if claims about failures and controls carry explicit evidence boundaries.

chapters.reader_question: How can a living technical work improve its explanations without silently making its claims sound better supported?
chapters.running_example: The repository-change experiment may strengthen a narrow attack-resistance result while the chapter-level architecture claim remains an argument.
chapters.strongest_objection: Support labels can create a false aura of rigor when the underlying evidence is stale, narrow, dependent, or poorly measured.
chapters.failure_story: A passing schema check is cited as if it proved runtime safety, and repeated prose gradually turns that narrow receipt into a broad claim.
chapters.evidence_that_would_change_the_conclusio: Independent audits showing that the transition system fails to prevent overclaiming, or that a simpler review protocol performs as well, would force redesign of the evidence model.
chapters.handoff: Claim discipline becomes operationally difficult when limited reviewers must judge stronger systems under time and access constraints.

chapters.reader_question: What must an oversight protocol record when reviewers have less capability, time, context, or independence than the system they judge?
chapters.running_example: An independent verifier reviews the repository patch with bounded context while an outcome audit checks the actual tree and test effects.
chapters.strongest_objection: Model debate, voting, or human approval may merely correlate errors and move responsibility without making judgments more reliable.
chapters.failure_story: Two nominally independent reviewers share the same poisoned context and approve a forged receipt with high confidence.
chapters.evidence_that_would_change_the_conclusio: Controlled oversight studies measuring no advantage over direct review, or showing worse correlated failure and operator burden, would narrow the proposed protocol family.
chapters.handoff: Oversight must remain effective when the system proposes changes to its own capabilities, evaluators, or governance machinery.

chapters.reader_question: Which changes may a capable system propose about itself, and which evidence and authority gates must remain outside that proposal?
chapters.running_example: The repository-change system proposes modifying its verifier, validation policy, or deployment workflow rather than an ordinary chapter file.
chapters.strongest_objection: A sufficiently capable optimizer may shape the tests, evidence, and reviewers used to approve its own improvement while satisfying every recorded field.
chapters.failure_story: A faster verifier is promoted using benchmarks it helped select, then silently stops detecting the attack that motivated the old verifier.
chapters.evidence_that_would_change_the_conclusio: Longitudinal governed-improvement trials showing that simpler change control prevents evaluator capture and preserves rollback equally well would reduce the need for specialized self-improvement gates.
chapters.handoff: The operational half of the book begins by compiling human intent into an executable contract that still carries those ceilings.

chapters.reader_question: How does an ambiguous human request become bounded work without inventing authority, success criteria, or hidden assumptions?
chapters.running_example: Translate a request to improve a book chapter into target files, allowed operations, evidence obligations, stop conditions, and approval requirements.
chapters.strongest_objection: Formal command contracts may give precise shape to a misunderstood intent and make a bad interpretation harder to notice.
chapters.failure_story: The system completes the literal patch while violating the user's actual constraint because uncertainty was converted into an unstated default.
chapters.evidence_that_would_change_the_conclusio: User studies and task evaluations showing that lighter conversational clarification produces fewer intent failures at lower burden would require a smaller contract surface.
chapters.handoff: A valid command still needs a plan whose dependencies, alternatives, verification points, and replan conditions remain inspectable.

chapters.reader_question: How can a plan coordinate work while remaining a revisable proposal rather than becoming implicit permission to act?
chapters.running_example: Represent the repository change as a DAG of source inspection, patch generation, independent verification, tests, rollback preparation, and release or refusal.
chapters.strongest_objection: Detailed plans can be expensive, brittle, and misleading when the environment changes faster than the plan can be validated.
chapters.failure_story: A stale plan executes a now-invalid step because its context and authorization dependencies were not rechecked at runtime.
chapters.evidence_that_would_change_the_conclusio: Matched dynamic-task studies showing that direct reactive control is safer and more efficient than explicit dependency plans would narrow when the planning layer should activate.
chapters.handoff: Planning quality is bounded by whether the system has enough context and verification capacity for the claims its steps depend on.

chapters.reader_question: When does having relevant context fail to provide enough comparison and review capacity to verify a risky claim?
chapters.running_example: The verifier sees the patch and tests but lacks the policy history needed to notice that a removed check was a deliberate security boundary.
chapters.strongest_objection: Context-adequacy labels may be subjective gates that add delay without reliably predicting missed contradictions.
chapters.failure_story: A concise summary preserves topic coverage but drops the single constraint that should have blocked deployment.
chapters.evidence_that_would_change_the_conclusio: Benchmarks showing no relationship between declared adequacy, review allocation, and contradiction detection would require replacing or sharply simplifying the adequacy model.
chapters.handoff: Verified work must then become durable enough that later reviewers can inspect its causes, effects, limits, and replay conditions.

chapters.reader_question: What must survive after a task so another person can reconstruct, challenge, reuse, or roll back the work?
chapters.running_example: Connect the request, source snapshot, plan, patch, verifier decision, test output, rollback result, and final disposition in one artifact graph.
chapters.strongest_objection: A complete-looking graph can still preserve fabricated lineage or overwhelm reviewers with records that nobody checks.
chapters.failure_story: The patch passes, but its test receipt belongs to a different commit and the mismatch remains hidden inside an unchallenged audit bundle.
chapters.evidence_that_would_change_the_conclusio: Challenge-sampling studies showing no improvement in fault discovery or replay over simpler provenance records would justify reducing graph scope.
chapters.handoff: The graph reaches its highest-risk edge when an authorized runtime adapter turns a proposed change into an external effect.

chapters.reader_question: Which narrow boundary should turn a plan into filesystem, network, deployment, financial, or physical effects?
chapters.running_example: A sandboxed adapter applies the verified patch only while its grant is current, records pre/post state, and retains an executable rollback handle.
chapters.strongest_objection: Approval and sandbox layers may encourage rubber-stamping while sophisticated tool chains find effect paths outside the declared adapter.
chapters.failure_story: A human approves a narrow file edit, but the adapter reuses the approval for a broader deployment and reports tool success as task success.
chapters.evidence_that_would_change_the_conclusio: Production traces showing that simpler least-privilege tool wrappers achieve equal revocation, rollback, and effect-observation performance would narrow the adapter contract.
chapters.handoff: Every control consumes compute, latency, attention, and reviewer capacity, so the architecture must account for governance rather than assume it is free.

chapters.reader_question: When does governance reduce risk enough to justify its cost, and when does it merely add burden that operators will bypass?
chapters.running_example: Compare governed and simpler repository-change routes on success, false accepts and rejects, latency, compute, reviewer work, rollback, and hidden residuals.
chapters.strongest_objection: Accounting can be manipulated by excluding hard-to-measure human burden, rare losses, transfer risk, or the cost of maintaining the governance system itself.
chapters.failure_story: A route wins on token cost while review queues grow, operators bypass gates, and an uncounted rollback failure moves the burden downstream.
chapters.evidence_that_would_change_the_conclusio: Representative workloads showing governance costs exceed avoided losses across risk tiers, or that routing cannot classify those tiers reliably, would require material simplification.
chapters.handoff: The integrated reference chapter now reconnects authority, plans, context, verification, effects, evidence, residuals, and improvement in one trace.

chapters.reader_question: Do the separate controls compose into one trace whose allowed, blocked, failed, and rolled-back paths remain intelligible?
chapters.running_example: Replay the complete governed repository change and its attacks from human intent through final release or refusal.
chapters.strongest_objection: A reference trace can be internally coherent while omitting the open-world behavior, concurrency, institutions, and adversaries that break a real deployment.
chapters.failure_story: Every component passes its local check, yet a stale authorization and mismatched receipt cross the handoffs and produce an unsafe release.
chapters.evidence_that_would_change_the_conclusio: End-to-end implementations showing persistent composition failures despite locally valid contracts would falsify specific interface assumptions and force architectural revision.
chapters.handoff: The final chapter applies the same trace discipline to the book itself so publication claims cannot outrun source, validation, and release reality.

chapters.reader_question: Can the manuscript demonstrate governed improvement by keeping its sources, claims, tests, releases, and public artifact mutually accountable?
chapters.running_example: Treat this critique remediation as a change trace with findings, controls, mutation tests, residuals, release gates, and explicit non-claims.
chapters.strongest_objection: The book's elaborate self-governance may optimize records about research rather than produce stronger research or a better reading experience.
chapters.failure_story: Local ledgers declare coherence while the deployed site exposes stale counts, proving that record quality did not control public reality.
chapters.evidence_that_would_change_the_conclusio: Independent editorial and research comparisons showing that the method adds maintenance cost without improving correction rate, reproducibility, or reader trust would require a smaller methodology.
chapters.handoff: The narrative route ends with open residuals: deployment attestation, immutable release publication, independent review, and continued empirical pressure on the three defended contributions.

omission_policy: The remaining canonical chapters are not deleted, demoted, or treated as unimportant. They remain full architecture-reference material and are routed from the closest selected narrative owner. A future reader release may merge or summarize their mechanisms only through reviewed derivative prose with preserved uncertainty and source boundaries.
review_gate: Generate the derivative from the current canonical manifest rather than copying a parallel manuscript.
Verify that every selected chapter exists, remains in canonical order, and carries its current core-claim reference.
Verify that every omitted chapter remains discoverable through the architecture-reference route.
Complete human continuity, repetition, accessibility, figure, and format review before calling the derivative a reader release.
Create an edition release record only for artifacts that were actually rendered and reviewed.
non_claims: Selection for the narrative spine does not promote a chapter-core support state.
Omission from the narrative spine does not reject, deprecate, or remove a canonical chapter.
A generated candidate is not a reviewed reader release, publication artifact, or independent editorial approval.
The orientation fields are editorial navigation, not new evidence or replacements for canonical claims.
